evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent: posthog.init, PostHogProvider, useFeatureFlagEnabled, posthog.identify, posthog.capture (JS); Posthog(api_key, host) and capture(distinct_id, event, properties) (Python). Hosts us.i.posthog.com / eu.i.posthog.com match known patterns. No version pin claimed but method names self-consistent.
evidence.executability: Code blocks parse. Issue: imports `flush` from `posthog` top-level (likely should be posthog_client.flush()); uses `Posthog | None` union syntax (needs Python 3.10+, claimed). Test imports posthog_client at module load before lifespan runs, so posthog_client is None — assertions guarded by `if posthog_client is not None` will silently skip. Frontend fetch uses relative '/signup' with no CORS/proxy config noted. Multiple non-obvious fixes needed.
evidence.completeness: (a) Key acquisition described in prose (project settings) without exact URL/menu path; (b) install commands present in bash; (c) env templates present for both frontend (VITE_) and backend (POSTHOG_); (d) backend code in fenced blocks; (e) frontend code in fenced blocks; (f) test commands implicit (pytest, vitest) but no explicit single test command block. Env var names consistent between template and code.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Numerous bracketed numeric citations [1][2]... but no actual URLs provided. No posthog.com/docs links anywhere. Citations are unresolvable references.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable: long-tail handling applied. Self-consistent + no version pin, gets 4 on correctness. Citation numerals without URLs = fabricated/unresolvable -> score 1.
